Page | Zebra Technologies | | | ## 1. **OVERVIEW** The following work instructions explains the workflow for vacuum sealing the moisture sensitive ESD components (BGAs, and ICs) # 2. **VACUUM SEALING OPERATION** - 1.When picking the partial (less than a full sealed tray/tube/reel) moisture sensitive ESD components like BGAs or ICs, the user will need to utilize a vacuum sealer to properly seal the components in a moisture protective ESD packaging. - 2. Pick the sealed tray/tube/reel and bring it to the ESD station, refer to ESD Sensitive Parts Handling. - 3. Open the sealed tray/tube/reel 1/16" below the seal level ![](_page_0_Picture_7.jpeg) ✓ Make sure to cut slightly below the seam to leave as much packaging for re-sealing after picking the partials. ![](_page_0_Picture_9.jpeg) - 4. Take out the required quantity of components out of the sealed packaging using the vacuum pen. Press on the button to create a vacuum and pick up the part. - 5. Place the components in a special tray, 1 part per a tray slot. ![](_page_0_Picture_12.jpeg) *This document contains proprietary information which is the exclusive property of Zebra Technologies Corp.* ## **CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION WORK INSTRUCTIONS** Zebra Technologies **VACUUM SEALING OPERATION Work Instruction No. Revision No. 02 Site Code WI03 Page 2 OF 4** 6. Place the tray in a moisture protective ESD bag together with the desiccant and a moisture-indicating card. ![](_page_1_Picture_2.jpeg) ![](_page_1_Picture_3.jpeg) 7. Place the bag on the vacuum sealing machine, make sure to thread the vacuum valve between the bag edges as displayed. ![](_page_1_Picture_5.jpeg) ![](_page_1_Picture_6.jpeg) 8. Turn on the compressor by pulling the red button out. ![](_page_1_Picture_8.jpeg)

Press on the pedal and wait for the vacuum machine to suck the air out of the packaging. ![](_page_2_Picture_4.jpeg) - 11. Release the pedal once the air is sucked out of the packaging, the light on the vacuum sealer will light up, indicating the heating process. - 12. Once the vacuum sealer bar moves up and releases the packaging, it will be sealed. ![](_page_2_Picture_7.jpeg) | CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION | | | | | |-----------------------------------------------|----------------------|--------------------|--|--| | WORK INSTRUCTIONS

Shut off the compressor by pushing the red button in. ![](_page_3_Picture_3.jpeg) - 15. Power off the vacuum sealer by pressing the switch. - ✓ Compressor and vacuum sealer should be shut down after each use! ## 3. **REFERENCES** | Document # | Document Title | |------------|-------------------------------------------------| | | Create and Print TX5 Bulk Parts Label Rev01.pdf | # 4. **REVISION HISTORY** The following table lists all revisions (including the original document) to this procedure, the date, and the reason for the revision. | Rev. | Rev.
